title: task
description: compact_cot.py:778-786 - Input values from XML are converted to wire format integers without range validation. Malformed or extreme values cause struct.error during encoding rather than clear validation errors during parsing. Example: lat=999 produces lat_microdeg=999_000_000 which exceeds int32 max. Course=400 produces course_cdeg=40000 (valid for uint16 but semantically wrong for 0-359.99 degrees). The error surfaces late (at encode time) with cryptic struct.error instead of early clear ValueError during parse.
